Off Calendar Aug-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, or in the alternative summary adjudication. Appearances: Jed Borghei, Esq. of Baron & Budd P.C. (310) 860-0476 x7, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Daniel B. Hoye, Esq. of McKenna Long & Aldridge LLP (415) 267-4094, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment and Summary Adjudication denied as to issues number 1 and 2. Triable issue of fact regarding exposure. (Hansen deposition.) Summary Adjudication granted as to issue number 3. Strict products liability has replaced the implied warranty claim where there is no privity. (See Alvarez vs. Felker Mfg. Co. (1964) 230 Cal.App. 2nd 987, 1005.) No triable issue of fact raised establishing an express warranty. Summary Adjudication granted as to issue 4, no triable issue of fact raised that defendant made a false representation to decedent. Summary Adjudication denied as to issues number 5 and 6. Exhibits to Brown declaration raise triable issue of fact that defendant had knowledge of the dangers of asbestos but continued to sell the product. Defendant's objections overruled. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
